fresh_facts.claim: US forces deployed thousands of additional troops to the Middle East as President Trump extended an indefinite ceasefire deadline for Iran.
The USS Rafael Peralta intercepted the tanker MT Stream en route to an Iranian port as part of a US campaign that stopped approximately 8 Iranian tankers total.
IRGC boats repelled a US Marines boarding attempt via radio warnings and boat interception.
President Trump issued a 3-day ultimatum threatening Iran's oil pipelines and ramped up economic sanctions.
US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned companies aiding Iranian airlines of penalties.
Israeli airstrikes targeted Hezbollah positions deep in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley on April 28 in response to Hezbollah drone attacks on IDF troops.
Israeli Prime Minister urged the US to maintain the Hormuz blockade and prepare for potential renewed military action if talks fail.
IRGC gunboats fired on a container ship and a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, per British military reports.
Approximately 50% of Iran's ballistic missile stockpile and launchers remain intact post-ceasefire.
Iran offered to reopen the Hormuz Strait but shelved nuclear talks until after war and shipping resolutions.
Trump rejected Iran's Hormuz reopening offer as unacceptable.
Iran restarted limited international flights from Tehran amid the US blockade.
U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan were canceled by Trump on or before April 27, 2026.
Iranian Foreign Minister Zarif and parliament speaker Qalibaf attended high-level meetings in Islamabad, signaling an intact chain of command.
Unconfirmed reports from April 27 note Supreme Leader Khamenei's possible serious injury, yet high-level attendance indicates leadership continuity.
The Iranian regime has been framing protesters as terrorists and seeking foreign repression aid, with arrests of security forces refusing orders in Kermanshah.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Betting markets speculate 7.5% odds of Iranian regime collapse by June 30, 2026.
Iran's political stability index stood at -1.58 in 2024, the lowest since 1996.
Over 700 arrests occurred post-ceasefire, with IRGC/Basij raids in ethnic regions, checkpoints, and fatal shootings reported in July 2025 near Hamedan.
Brutal crackdowns on ongoing protests include internet blackouts and Starlink confiscations, with 800-5,000 Iraqi-backed fighters deployed.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Israeli forces seized weapons, drones, and mines in southern Lebanon on April 28 during border security operations.
Active warfare continues between Israel and Lebanese forces despite ceasefire, with Israeli responses to Hezbollah.
Mossad assessments highlight risks of US-Iran diplomatic collapse and prepare for potential renewed military conflict.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an unprecedented convergence of existential pressures: Trump rejected Iran's Hormuz reopening offer and issued a 3-day ultimatum threatening pipeline destruction while deploying thousands of additional troops; the US blockade has intercepted ~8 Iranian tankers and expanded to 39 vessels; IRGC repelled a US Marine boarding attempt but continues destabilizing attacks on shipping; domestic unrest persists with mass arrests, security force defections, and foreign militia deployments; and diplomatic channels have completely collapsed with high-level regime officials still functionally present but facing potential succession uncertainty.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 29, 2026, Trump rejected Iran's conditional Hormuz reopening offer as unacceptable and deployed thousands of additional US troops while maintaining the indefinite ceasefire deadline ultimatum, marking the definitive collapse of diplomatic negotiations.
